title: pkg/machine/e2e: skip rosetta test
body:
We disabled it in the machine os image so it can no longer be used currently.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@baude @mheon PTAL, the machine os was merged and I missed this test depends on rosetta so machine CI is broken until this gets merged.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

ssh to running machine and check proxy settings
```
Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: ssh: unexpected packet in response to channel open: <nil>
```
In the proxy test, that one is new I see it on https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26292 as well I though it was start a flake but given it fails in this exact tests I wonder if something "bad" got into the image now that we are using the latest coreos all of the sudden...
Ok I found a workaround, something is very broken in the new image that setting a basic env var via environment.d  causes boot failures.
